questions.question: Each wing of your aircraft is 16' long with a 3.5' chord. If the maximum takeoff weight is 2,668 LBS, what is the maximum wing loading of the aircraft?

You are currently flying at 6,500 feet, and your destination airport is 15 nautical miles away at an elevation of 1,750 feet. If you start your descent now at 550 fpm, what ground speed should you fly at to maintain a steady descent to your destination?

You are at an altitude of 7,000' and lose your engine. There is an airport a few miles ahead with an elevation of 1,640'. You pitch for best glide and your GPS shows a ground speed of 75 kts. Your vertical speed indicator shows -1,350. How far can you glide?
Reference the previous question. Since math is your specialty, what is the percentage difference in price per square inch?

choices.text: Medium is 7.5% more expensive than the Large
Medium is 7% more expensive than the Large
Medium is 19% more expensive than the Large
Large is 21% more expensive than the Medium

questions.correctChoice: Medium is 7.5% more expensive than the Large
questions.context: Referenced question 35: You're hungry and trying to determine the best pizza deal. A Medium 12" diameter pizza is $14.99 and a Large 14" diameter pizza is $18.99. How much bigger is the large pizza?

questions.question: You want to visit your friend Josh just outside of Billings. When you check in with KBIL tower, they clear you to land on Runway 10R (3,800' long). You know the approach end of the runway is 3,662' MSL and the departure end is 3,614' MSL. What is the slope of the runway?

Having flown 230 statute miles at an average ground speed of 138 knots, what is the fuel burn in gallons per hour if the fuel tanks (27 gallons usable) were topped off prior to departure and the quantity currently indicates 1/2 full?
At the end of a 288 NM cross-country flight, you refill the airplane with 19.4 gallons of fuel. If your average groundspeed was 103 knots, what was your average fuel burn rate?
What percentage of the total distance of the flight is spent descending?
questions.context: Use the following information to solve questions 36-40. An aircraft leaves their departure airport (Elev. 1,076') on a true course of 160° for 114 NM to their destination airport (Elev. 661'). Departure winds are 185° at 12 knots; destination winds are calm. Winds aloft at a cruise altitude of 7,500' MSL are 285° at 21 knots. [[TABLE]]
